summary: PR #2498 focused on improving `src/melee/lb/lbmemory` matching by adding structured allocator-related fields, decompiling relocation/init routines, introducing string/data references used by the matched code, and clarifying verified `Handle.x8_hi` behavior. The PR body reports the TU reaching 10/12 matched functions, with `lbMemory_80014FC8` and `lbMemory_80015320` still imperfect. Bot report records 4 new matches and one unmatched-function improvement.

key_files.role: Primary decomp/matching work: structured allocator fields, lbmemory relocation/init routines, string references, assert/report string use, AR/devcom includes, alarm/devcom relocation flow, and a scoped dont_inline pragma around `lbMemory_8001564C`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbMemory_8001564C implementation added with #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline on/#pragma pop
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists new matches for lbMemory_8001564C, fn_80015184, .data, .sdata and improvement for lbMemory_80015320

key_files.role: Header comment clarification for `Handle.x8_hi`, changing it from an uncertain question to documented dual behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `// is this hi or size?` changed to `// Arena high bound for heap handles; allocation size for child handles.`

what_changed.text: Replaced raw padding in `struct Allocator` with typed substructures/arrays: `MemEntry x8_mem[0x83]`, `Handle x638_heap[6]`, and `LBMgr x6A0_mgr`, while preserving allocator size with static asserts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Allocator fields changed from u8 padding to typed x8_mem/x638_heap/x6A0_mgr

what_changed.text: Changed allocator storage from a source-local static `g_alloc` to global symbol `lbMemory_804318B0` plus `#define g_alloc lbMemory_804318B0`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `static struct Allocator g_alloc;` replaced by `struct Allocator lbMemory_804318B0; #define g_alloc lbMemory_804318B0`

what_changed.text: Added local and SDATA string objects for assert/report text and converted multiple `__FILE__`/literal assert and `OSReport` uses to those symbols.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: .data and .sdata reported as new 100% matches

what_changed.text: Implemented or improved relocation/init routines: `lbMemory_80015320` was added as a decompiled relocation routine and `lbMemory_8001564C` was added as an init routine using AR allocation/free and free-list initialization.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: decompiles relocation and init routines

what_changed.text: Cleaned speculative comments from existing lbmemory functions and replaced the uncertain `Handle.x8_hi` header comment with a more specific behavior description.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: cleans speculative comments while keeping only verified Handle field behavior
raw_diff: removal of comments such as `this is weird.. i'm missing something`, `malloc`, and `same as ...`

what_changed.text: Converted the alarm delay in `fn_80015184` from raw tick constant/null callback shape to `OSMillisecondsToTicks(3)` and `fn_80015184`, and changed manager access from byte-offset pointer math to `&g_alloc.x6A0_mgr`.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `OSSetAlarm(&temp_r3->alarm, 0x10624DD3, NULL)` replaced by `OSSetAlarm(&temp_r3->alarm, OSMillisecondsToTicks(3), fn_80015184)`

smart_moves.text: The PR used recovered struct fields to replace opaque allocator padding and offset casts where possible, making manager and free-list accesses more reviewable while still preserving documented struct sizes.
The PR body explicitly separated completed matching progress from remaining partial matches and named residual mismatch causes for the two remaining functions.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Remaining section lists `lbMemory_80014FC8: 99.324326%` and `lbMemory_80015320: 96.106800%` with saved-register/instruction-scheduling differences

smart_moves.text: Narrow verification was reported in the PR body and corroborated by the bot progress report for the touched `main/melee/lb/lbmemory` unit.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `python configure.py && ninja`; pre-commit checks including match regression checks
text_corpus issue_comment: GALE01 report with matched-code/data deltas and item-level changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: When allocator padding resolves into repeated entries or manager subobjects, introducing small local structs and arrays can expose natural field access and reduce raw pointer arithmetic, but size/static assertions should accompany the recovery.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `struct MemEntry`, `struct LBMgr`, and typed `struct Allocator` fields
raw_diff: static asserts for `MemEntry` and allocator size

decomp_lessons.lesson: For PRs that add data/string symbols to support matching, record whether the PR evidence ties them to matched code/data sections rather than treating them as general style.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: adds string references used by matched code

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a function remains non-matching after source reconstruction, preserve the specific mismatch mode and score rather than claiming full closure.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: remaining percentages and saved-register/instruction-scheduling note

naming_conventions.name: MemEntry
naming_conventions.meaning: Recovered allocator memory-entry structure with next/lo/hi-style fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `struct MemEntry { struct MemEntry* x0_next; void* x4_lo; void* x8_hi; };`

naming_conventions.name: LBMgr
naming_conventions.meaning: Recovered relocation/transfer manager subobject containing `OSAlarm`, source/destination pointers, size/offset, callback argument, and callback.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: `struct LBMgr` fields and use in `fn_80015184`/`lbMemory_80015320`

naming_conventions.name: Handle.x8_hi
naming_conventions.meaning: PR evidence updates the field comment to describe dual usage: arena high bound for heap handles and allocation size for child handles.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbmemory.h comment changed to `Arena high bound for heap handles; allocation size for child handles.`

naming_conventions.name: lbMemory_804318B0 / g_alloc
naming_conventions.kind: global allocator symbol plus local alias macro
naming_conventions.meaning: Allocator storage was named as `lbMemory_804318B0` and referenced through `g_alloc` macro in this PR; this is evidence of the PR shape, not an accepted global renaming convention.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use typed subobjects to recover offset-based accesses.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: `fn_80015184` changes from `(struct LBMgr*) ((u8*) &g_alloc + 0x6A0)` to `&g_alloc.x6A0_mgr`, and allocator padding becomes typed arrays/substructs.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use natural loops for repeated free-list initialization.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: `lbMemory_8001564C` initializes `x8_mem` with a `for (i = 0; i < 0x82; i++)` loop before terminating the list.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use SDK/helper macros for time constants when matching supports it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: `OSSetAlarm` uses `OSMillisecondsToTicks(3)` instead of raw constant `0x10624DD3` in `fn_80015184` and the newly added relocation path.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped pragma use was introduced around `lbMemory_8001564C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The init routine is wrapped in `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop`; the PR body/bot report supports the function becoming matched, but no review discussion explains why the pragma was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: String/data symbol materialization was used for assert/report text and section matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: The PR introduces named local/SDATA strings and routes assert/report arguments through them; bot report shows .data and .sdata matching gains. This is high-risk under current standards unless tied to data ownership evidence, so keep as PR-specific tactic rather than broad recommendation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: string symbol definitions and assert/report conversions

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code 67.01% (+0.02%, +924 bytes) and matched data 39.30% (+0.02%, +216 bytes), with 4 new matches: `lbMemory_8001564C`, `fn_80015184`, `.data`, `.sdata`; and one improvement for `lbMemory_80015320` to 96.11%.

follow_up_queries: lbmemory lbMemory_80015320 saved-register allocation instruction scheduling

lbMemory_804318B0 g_alloc define alias allocator symbol
lbmemory assert string symbols .data .sdata lbl_803BA2C0 lbMemory_804D3788

There are no human review comments or reviews in the supplied slice, so reviewer intent and acceptance rationale are unavailable.
The PR is closed with `merged_at: null`; do not infer merge/acceptance status from the diff.

String-symbol materialization and `#define g_alloc lbMemory_804318B0` conflict with or at least raise questions under current global standards, but the historical PR evidence says they were used for matched code/data in this slice. Treat as source-update/review candidates, not accepted standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed allocator subobjects can replace padding when size evidence is preserved
accepted_candidate_records.text: In `src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c`, PR #2498 replaced allocator padding with `MemEntry`, `Handle` array, and `LBMgr` fields and kept size static asserts. This is a reusable example of typed-field recovery improving reviewability while preserving layout evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: static asserts for struct sizes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Record partial-match residuals explicitly
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2498 reports remaining function scores and the residual mismatch class for `lbMemory_80014FC8` and `lbMemory_80015320` instead of describing them as matched: control-flow/size shape matched, but saved-register allocation and instruction scheduling still differed.

source_update_candidates.title: lbmemory allocator layout evidence from PR #2498
source_update_candidates.text: `src/melee/lb/lbmemory.c` in PR #2498 modeled allocator storage as `struct Allocator` with `MemEntry x8_mem[0x83]`, `Handle x638_heap[6]`, `LBMgr x6A0_mgr`, and total size 0x6F0. Curator should verify against current source/symbol metadata before promoting as a path fact.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful local layout fact, but PR was closed unmerged and current source may differ.

source_update_candidates.title: Handle.x8_hi dual-use comment from PR #2498
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2498 changed `Handle.x8_hi` comment to `Arena high bound for heap handles; allocation size for child handles.` Curator should compare with current header/source before adopting this as a path fact.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: only verified Handle field behavior
source_update_candidates.reason: Concrete field-behavior claim in PR evidence, but no review acceptance and PR was not merged.

source_update_candidates.title: Require extra scrutiny for string-symbol materialization in matching PRs
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2498 used named local/SDATA strings for assert/report text and bot report showed `.data`/`.sdata` matching gains. Under current standards, this should remain high-scrutiny: only keep string/data symbol materialization when section ownership, symbol metadata, or explicit PR scope supports it, and do not generalize from a single code-matching PR.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added string symbols and converted assert/report uses
human_pr_text: string references used by matched code
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR is a useful cautionary example for curator review, not proof of an accepted broad exception.

source_update_candidates.title: Pragma use around matched init routine needs explicit rationale
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2498 wrapped `lbMemory_8001564C` in `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, `#pragma pop`, and the bot reported that function as a new 100% match. The slice does not include rationale or adjacent-regression details; curator may want standards/examples requiring PR authors to state why scoped pragmas are necessary.
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards already discourage pragma use without evidence; this PR provides a concrete review example with incomplete rationale in the slice.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `#define g_alloc lbMemory_804318B0` as an accepted naming or aliasing convention; current standards disallow speculative define aliases for globals, and this slice has no review acceptance.
Do not claim `lbMemory_80015320` matched; PR body and bot report both show it remained partial at about 96.11%.
Do not infer that the PR was merged or accepted; metadata says CLOSED and `merged_at` is null.
Do not infer reviewer feedback beyond the bot report; the slice has zero review comments and zero reviews.
